title: 10 best herbal capsules for frozen shoulder

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which target the inflammation that occurs in the joint capsule, leading to stiffness and pain. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as limited mobility and difficulty moving the arm. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the joint from further damage, promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_properties: C curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.

COX-2 inhibition: Turmeric's ability to inhibit COX-2 enzyme activity reduces the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain, swelling, and inflammation, thereby providing relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue damage in frozen shoulder, leading to improved joint health and reduced pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A phenolic compound that may help alleviate pain and inflammation by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing tissue damage and promoting healing.

Beta-Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties by activating the CB2 receptor, which helps reduce inflammation and pain in the affected shoulder joint.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Curcuma longa are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder as they contain the highest concentration of curcuminoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used in capsule form for frozen shoulder due to their bioactive compounds, including curcumin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.

Barks: Although less common, the barks of Curcuma longa are occasionally used in capsule form for frozen shoulder as they are believed to contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried curcuma longa root powder from a reputable supplier or cultivated source.
Weigh 400mg of the powder and mix it with 100mg of vegetable cellulose powder.
Add 50mg of silica as a capsule filler and mix until well combined.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with the mixture.
Seal the capsules and store them in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The capsules also contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural analgesic effects, helping to alleviate discomfort and improve range of motion. Additionally, ginger's warming nature helps increase blood flow to the area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, helping to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These bioactive compounds, particularly gingerol and shogaol, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Shogaols: Shogaols are also known to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may contribute to the relief of pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected shoulder joint, thereby improving mobility and function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for its anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: Used for its warming and pain-relieving properties to help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Barks: Used for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and swelling in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 milligrams of dried zingiber officinale root powder and 5 milligrams of silica gel.
Mix the powder and silica gel together in a small bowl for 1 minute.
Add 1 milliliter of gelatin solution to the mixture and stir for 2 minutes.
Fill a capsule with 250 milligrams of the mixture and seal the capsule immediately.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and swelling. The capsaicin present in bell peppers blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This helps alleviate stiffness and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, bell pepper's antioxidants help repair damaged tissues and promote healthy collagen production, further aiding in the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Contains curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected joint.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which may help relieve pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Boswellia (Boswellia serrata): Contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility in conditions such as osteoarthritis.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a capsaicinoid and a potent analgesic, helps with frozen shoulder by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing pain and inflammation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, a type of phenolic compound, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with frozen shoulder, thereby promoting healing and recovery.

Carotenoids: Carotenoids, including beta-carotene and lycopene, have antioxidant properties that help protect joints from oxidative damage, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair, all of which may contribute to alleviating frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are commonly consumed as a spice or used in various dishes, providing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used in various folk remedies, including reducing pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Capsicum seeds contain capsaicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which may help with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh capsicum annuum pods at peak ripeness and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for 7 days.
Grind 200g of dried capsicum annuum pods into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 100g of the capsicum annuum powder with 100g of gelatin and 100ml of water to form a uniform gel.
Pour the gel mixture into empty gelatin capsules and fill each capsule to the top.
Store the filled capsicum annuum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain salicin, a natural compound that mimics the pain-relieving properties of aspirin. When used topically, salicin reduces inflammation and relieves pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and swelling. By reducing inflammation and pain, white willow capsules can help alleviate stiffness and restricted mobility associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in frozen shoulder patients.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The salicin in white willow capsules also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility.
Pain modulation: White willow's medicinal properties may also modulate pain signals to the brain, helping to reduce the perception of pain and discomfort in patients with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A glycoside compound that acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant that inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Salicylic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder joint and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Barks: Barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Stems: Stems are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected joint, helping to relieve frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried salix alba bark from a trusted supplier or prepare it yourself by drying fresh material at 50°c for 3 hours.
Grind the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a food processor for 5 minutes.
Mix 200g of the salix alba powder with 100g of a filler material like microcrystalline cellulose in a clean bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 0.5g of the salix alba mixture each using a capsule filler or scoop.
Store the finished herbal salix alba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain harpagoside, a bioactive compound that reduces inflammation and relieves pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of devil's claw help to alleviate stiffness and discomfort in the shoulder joint, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. Additionally, the herb's analgesic properties work to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. This natural remedy can be particularly beneficial for those seeking an alternative or complementary approach to traditional treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Devil's Claw capsules contain iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in cases of frozen shoulder, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced discomfort.

Analgesic properties: The anti-nociceptive (pain-relieving) effects of Devil's Claw capsules help to alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to perform daily activities and reducing the risk of chronic pain development.

Antioxidant activity: Devil's Claw capsules contain antioxidants that help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to frozen shoulder development; by neutralizing free radicals, these antioxidants may help slow or prevent joint degeneration.
remedy_constituents: Harpgoside: Harpgoside is a phenolic glycoside that helps reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, which contribute to the development of frozen shoulder symptoms.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, procumbide): Iridoid glycosides, such as procumbide, exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Alkaloids (specifically, harpagosine and harpagonine): Alkaloids like harpagosine and harpagonine have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected shoulder joint.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Harpagophytum procumbens are primarily used to make capsules due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as harpagoside.
Roots: The roots are also used for their analgesic and anti-rheumatic properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Roots: Additionally, the roots of Harpagophytum procumbens contain anti-oxidant properties that help in protecting the muscles and joints from damage caused by oxidative stress, contributing to the treatment of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of dried roots of harpagophytum procumbens in the morning after 2-3 hours of sunrise.
Clean the roots thoroughly by washing them with water and then drying them in the sun for 2 hours.
Grind 200-300 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a ratio of 2:1 for 10 minutes.
Fill 100-200 capsules with the mixture and seal them in a clean environment using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the affected area. The capsules have been shown to stimulate blood flow, promote collagen synthesis, and decrease stiffness in the shoulder joint. As a result, they can help alleviate symptoms such as limited mobility, numbness, and tingling sensations associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, maidenhair tree capsules may also enhance the body's natural healing processes, promoting faster recovery from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces joint pain and inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Ginger has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help reduce pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint, common symptoms of frozen shoulder.
Willow Bark (Salix alba): Willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that reduces pain and inflammation, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: It helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby improving joint mobility and reducing pain.
Ginkgolides: They help by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as leukotrienes, which contribute to the development of frozen shoulder.
Quercetin: It helps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba, as they contain the highest concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to aid in reducing inflammation and improving blood flow.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are also used, as they contain a high concentration of ginkgolides, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used, as they are believed to contain compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, although less research is available on the effectiveness of barks compared to leaves and seeds.
remedy_recipe: Grind 250g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 100g of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin in a bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin and glycerin.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture using a capsule filler.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a powerful adaptogenic herb that reduces inflammation and stress in the body, which are often underlying causes of this condition. By calming the nervous system and reducing cortisol levels, ashwagandha helps to relax the muscles and tendons surrounding the affected joint, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder, promoting a faster and more comfortable recovery.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help reduce inflammation and stress, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant and Free Radical Scavenging Activities: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the affected joint, promoting healing and reducing oxidative stress.

Anxiolytic and Relaxation-Inducing Properties: Ashwagandha's anxiolytic effects can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is essential for managing pain and improving range of motion in patients with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides, a class of alkaloids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Withaferin A: Withaferin A, a specific withanolide, has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder, such as stiffness and limited mobility.

C-witha-2-enolide (CWE): CWE, another withanolide, has been found to have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected joint, promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder due to their high content of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder because they contain flavonoids and other antioxidants that help reduce pain and inflammation.

Seeds: The seeds of Withania somnifera are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder due to their high content of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Clean and sanitize the workspace to ensure a hygienic environment for capsule preparation.
Grind 1-2 grams of dried withania somnifera roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the withania somnifera powder with a small amount of starch or filler to facilitate capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the withania somnifera mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the prepared capsules in a cool dark place to preserve their medicinal properties for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken regularly, it reduces inflammation and relaxes stiff muscles, thus alleviating the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has natural analgesic properties that help to numb the area, providing temporary relief from discomfort and making it an effective complementary therapy for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Cinnamon's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected joints, which can contribute to pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid and cinnamaldehyde, can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Pain relief properties: Cinnamon contains compounds that have been shown to possess analgesic and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate chronic pain, stiffness, and limited range of motion commonly experienced in frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Coumarin: As a phenolic compound, coumarin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate pain and improve mobility in individuals with frozen shoulder.

remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make capsules due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Utilized in capsules to provide relief from frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and swelling.
Rhyzomes: Used in capsule form for frozen shoulder as they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and stiffness in the affected shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground powder with 1000 grams of powdered cellulose in a mixing bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and set the machine to produce 2000 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for six hours.
Label the capsules with the contents and date, and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The capsules contain parthenolide, a natural compound that inhibits the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. By reducing inflammation, feverfew capsules can help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder, such as limited mobility and range of motion. remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain through its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Tanacetol (Camphor): This terpene helps with frozen shoulder by reducing pain and inflammation through its local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: They are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves at a low temperature of 35-40 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Grind 500 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 grams of the powder with 250 grams of gelatin in a bowl to create a gelatin mixture.
Fill 1000 empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the gelatin mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the filled tanacetum parthenium capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This reduces pain and swelling in the affected joint, allowing for increased mobility and reduced stiffness. Additionally, St John's Wort capsules have been shown to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by chronic pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. By addressing both physical and emotional aspects of the condition, these supplements can provide comprehensive relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

Flavonoids: These bioactive compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help combat oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by the condition, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation.
Hypericin: This active compound has anti-spasmodic effects, which can help relax muscle spasms and reduce stiffness in the shoulder joint, improving range of motion and reducing discomfort.

Hypericin: A naphthodianthrone with antioxidant properties that helps to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing of damaged tissues in the shoulder joint.

Flavonoids (specifically, rutin and quercetin): A group of phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Flowers: They contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them potentially useful in reducing pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Seeds: They are a source of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility, making them a potential treatment for frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in their entirety.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10-15 grams of the powdered plant material with 20-25 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400-500 milligrams of the plant-gelatin mixture using a spoon.
Label and store the herbal hypericum perforatum capsules in a cool dark place immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they provide natural anti-inflammatory relief, which is essential in reducing pain and stiffness associated with this condition. Lavender's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to calm the inflammatory response, promoting relaxation and reducing tension in the muscles and joints. Additionally, lavender's soothing properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, common contributors to frozen shoulder symptoms, allowing for a more effective recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain, swelling, and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort.

Muscle relaxant: Lavender's calming effects on muscle tissue can help reduce muscle spasms and tension, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness in the frozen shoulder.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning for maximum essential oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 200 milligrams of the ground lavandula angustifolia powder and seal them securely.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms. Muscle relaxant properties: Chamomile contains flavonoids like apigenin and quercetin that have been shown to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which is particularly beneficial in reducing stiffness and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder.

Seeds: Chamomile seeds contain essential oils and flavonoids that help reduce swelling and relax muscle spasms, which can alleviate the stiffness and limited mobility of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered chamomile with 5 grams of virgin coconut oil in a small bowl to create a paste.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the chamomile-coconut oil mixture and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and stiffness associated with the condition. Glycerrhetic acid: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, while also reducing joint swelling and inflammation.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes contain a compound called isoliquiritigenin, which is known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a reputable supplier.
Mix the powder with 500ml of boiling distilled water in a stainless steel pot for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass measuring cup and let it cool to room temperature.
Combine the cooled liquid extract with 100g of gelatin capsules and fill to capacity using an encapsulation machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at a controlled room temperature of 20-25 degrees celsius.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis roots and leaves in sufficient quantities for the desired dosage.
Grind the dried herb mixture into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered herb with 125mg of gelatin capsules for each capsule to be filled.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with the herb-gelatin mixture and seal them tightly by hand or using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or in the refrigerator for longer preservation.

remedy_recipe: Grind 200 grams of dried boswellia serrata herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground boswellia serrata powder with 100 grams of potato starch in a bowl until well combined for 2 minutes.
Add 50 grams of glycerin to the mixture and stir until a uniform paste forms within 1 minute.
Fill gelatin capsules with one-half teaspoon of the herbal mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency for 15 minutes.
Store the boswellia serrata capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joints. The herb has been traditionally used to relieve joint discomfort and improve flexibility, making it a natural remedy for frozen shoulder. Additionally, cleavers may help reduce inflammation and promote healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area, leading to faster recovery and improved mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers' bioactive compounds, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation in various tissues, which can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: Cleavers contain antioxidants that may help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the risk of chronic conditions like frozen shoulder.

Muscle relaxant and analgesic properties: The phytochemicals present in cleavers capsules may help relieve muscle tension and pain by inhibiting the activity of enzymes responsible for pain transmission, providing relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cleavers have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cleavers contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest galium aparine leaves and stems in late summer or early fall when plant is flowering.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air dry for 7 days.
Grind 30g of dried galium aparine into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part galium aparine powder with 2 parts organic gelatin in a small bowl to form a paste.
Fill empty #0 capsules with approximately 250mg of the herbal paste and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules help with frozen shoulder because they contain flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling in the affected joint. The capsules also stimulate blood flow to the area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. Additionally, yarrow's antispasmodic effects relax tense muscles and connective tissue, allowing for greater range of motion and flexibility. By addressing the underlying causes of frozen shoulder, yarrow capsules provide natural relief from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and pain in the shoulder joint caused by frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidant compounds can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage to tissues, which may contribute to the development of frozen shoulder and other musculoskeletal conditions.

Stimulant and vasodilatory effects: Yarrow's stimulatory and dilating properties can help improve blood circulation and reduce swelling in the affected area, which may alleviate pain, stiffness, and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality achillea millefolium leaves and flowers at dawn for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator on low for 12 hours.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder within minutes of drying.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of water to create a tincture that is left to steep for 2 weeks.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the filtered tincture solution and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for frozen shoulder are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with this condition. They work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area. The best herbal capsules for frozen shoulder include Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Withania somnifera, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Boswellia serrata, Capsicum annuum, Achillea millefolium, Arnica montana, Centella asiatica, and Equisetum arvense. If left untreated, frozen shoulder can severely impact daily life, leading to limited mobility, chronic pain, and reduced productivity, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal capsules are fibromyalgia, osteoarthritis, and tendonitis. These conditions often exhibit symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and limited mobility. Herbal supplements like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate these symptoms. Capsules containing a blend of herbs may also include ingredients like devil's claw, white willow, and ashwagandha to provide additional relief from chronic pain and discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with frozen shoulder is Turmeric, Boswellia, and Ginger. Turmeric contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and pain. Boswellia, also known as frankincense, has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties that ease stiffness and improve mobility. Ginger, with its anti-inflammatory compounds, enhances the healing process and reduces pain. Taking these herbal capsules in a balanced ratio can provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, promoting flexibility and reducing discomfort. Consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplements.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain ginger, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces pain and stiffness associated with this condition. The warm and soothing properties of ginger relax the muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. As a result, patients experience improved mobility and reduced discomfort, making it easier to perform daily activities and regain normal shoulder function. Regular use of Zingiber officinale capsules may also aid in reducing the duration and severity of frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to the relief of pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder patients.
Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help alleviate pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they are rich in gingerols and shogaols, compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain volatile oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder.

Buds: Buds are used because they have a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, compared to mature rhizomes, making them more effective in reducing inflammation and pain in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Dry the zingiber officinale roots in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the zingiber officinale powder with 125mg of a natural binding agent in a glass bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the zingiber officinale mixture using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal zingiber officinale capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a key compound in these capsules, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, curcumin's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve joint mobility makes it an effective natural remedy for frozen shoulder. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa capsules may also promote faster healing and recovery, making it a valuable adjunct to conventional treatment options.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the pain and stiffness associated with the condition.
Tumerones: They help with frozen shoulder by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing pain and improving joint mobility.
Volatile oils (sesquiterpenes): They help with frozen shoulder by providing anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate pain and stiffness in the affected joint.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain high levels of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.

Roots: Roots are used because they contain other bioactive compounds such as turmerones and sesquiterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain various bioactive compounds like flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried curcuma longa root with 2-3% curcumin content from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried root into fine powder using a spice grinder at 2-3 minutes.
Mix 500 mg of the ground powder with 100 mg of vegetable cellulose and 20 mg of silicon dioxide in a blending bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and fill 60-80 capsules in 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing. The herb, also known as Ashwagandha, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, it enhances the body's natural recovery process, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced discomfort. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting relaxation, Withania somnifera capsules support the body's natural ability to recover from frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by modulating the body's response to stress and promoting tissue repair.

Saponins: Saponins in Withania somnifera may help to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility in frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.

Alkaloids (Withanine and Catecholamines): These compounds help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder by modulating the body's response to pain and promoting relaxation and reduced muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: They are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which aid in reducing pain and promoting recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried withania somnifera roots from a reliable source within 3 months.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 10 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground withania somnifera powder with 500 milligrams of starch and 500 milligrams of cellulose.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture within 15 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root extract present in these capsules reduces inflammation and soothes the affected joint, easing pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes helps to reduce swelling and promote healing, making it a potential natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of frozen shoulder. This can lead to improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Flavonoids: These phytochemicals possess antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing in the affected area.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help alleviate pain and improve joint mobility in frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizic acid-rich roots are used to make capsules for frozen shoulder because of their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin present in the roots helps in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of glycyrrhizin in roots help in reducing stiffness and improving mobility in frozen shoulder patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly.
Grind 150 grams of the cleaned roots into fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 50 grams of starch or cellulose as a filler.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder-filler mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container and seal the container immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata capsules helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and inflammation in the affected shoulder joint. The capsules contain boswellic acids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Regular use of Boswellia serrata capsules may also improve range of motion and flexibility in the shoulder joint, making it easier to perform daily activities without discomfort. This natural remedy can provide relief and support for individuals with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Acylgeraninoids (also known as Boswellic acids): Inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, reducing pain and inflammation in the affected shoulder joint.
Phenolics (specifically, 3-acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid): Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties essential for relieving frozen shoulder symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain boswellic acids, which help in reducing inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: Boswellia serrata barks are also a rich source of boswellic acids, used to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of boswellia serrata roots and dry them for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Grind 50 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Add 200 ml of water to the bowl and heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes.
Fill 100 empty vegetarian capsules with the cooled mixture using a capsule filler for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected joint. The capsaicin present in these capsules has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax the muscles and reduce pain. Additionally, the increased blood flow helps to promote the healing process by bringing oxygen and nutrients to the affected area, allowing for faster recovery and improved mobility. This can help to break the cycle of pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: It helps with frozen shoulder by reducing pain and inflammation through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which block the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.

Capsaicinoids: They help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain through their ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing the severity of inflammation.

Carotenoids (including Beta-Carotene): They help with frozen shoulder by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation through their antioxidant properties, which protect cells from damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Used to make capsules as they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds and are rich in antioxidants.
Leaves: Used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried capsicum annuum at 60 mesh size in 10 minutes.
Pulverize the dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder in 10 minutes.
Mix 1g of the fine powder with 4g of starch and 2g of gelatin in 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine in 10 minutes.
Dry the filled capsules in a temperature and humidity controlled environment for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce pain and swelling associated with the condition. The capsules also contain antioxidants that promote healing and reduce oxidative stress in the affected joint. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Achillea millefolium capsules can improve range of motion and reduce stiffness in the shoulder, making it easier to perform daily activities and promoting overall recovery from frozen shoulder. This natural remedy offers a holistic approach to managing the condition.
remedy_constituents: Essential oils (specifically sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Flavonoids (specifically rutin and quercetin): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected joint.

Coumarins (specifically scopoletin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected joint, improving range of motion and function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Flowers: Utilized for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects to alleviate pain and discomfort in frozen shoulder patients.
Roots: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to promote healing and reduce inflammation in the affected shoulder joint.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a clean environment at dawn.
Dry the harvested material at 40c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10g of dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 0.5g of the powder with 5g of vegetable cellulose powder in a small bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties of Arnica montana, such as arnicoside A and B, can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, the antioxidant properties can help repair damaged tissues, improving range of motion and promoting a faster recovery. Many people have reported relief from frozen shoulder symptoms after using Arnica montana capsules.
remedy_constituents: Acidic sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Arnica montana have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Arnica montana have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation, and promote tissue repair in frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are often used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

Roots: The roots of Arnica montana are frequently used in herbal supplements due to their high content of caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried arnica montana flowers by cutting them from the plant at the peak of flowering.
Dry the harvested flowers further by spreading them on a paper bag in a warm dry place for 3 days.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder at 1500 rpm for 1 minute.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 teaspoon of the powdered arnica montana, approximately 250mg per capsule.
Seal the capsules by hand or using an automatic capsule filler at a speed of 10 capsules per minute.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain triterpenoids and saponins that enhance collagen synthesis and improve joint mobility. These compounds also reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, which is essential for recovery from frozen shoulder. Additionally, Centella asiatica has anti-oxidant properties that help protect the joint from further damage. By taking Centella asiatica capsules, individuals with frozen shoulder may experience improved range of motion and reduced pain, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Amenacina (a phenolic compound): It has shown to have antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, promoting healing and recovery.
Triterpenoid asiatic acid: It has been found to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, helping to reduce the severity of frozen shoulder symptoms and improve mobility.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for frozen shoulder treatment.
Roots: Utilized for their triterpenoid content, particularly asiaticoside, which has been shown to improve tissue repair and reduce inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Employed for their high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of fresh leaves of centella asiatica from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Dry the harvested leaves in a cool and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 2 grams of the ground herb powder with 2 grams of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine or a spoon.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense capsules helps with frozen shoulder because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also promote blood flow, which aids in the repair and regeneration of damaged tissues. Additionally, Equisetum arvense has been traditionally used to relax muscles and improve range of motion, making it easier to move the affected shoulder and shoulder blade. This can help to break the cycle of pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected tissues.
Saponins: These help by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Silicates: These help by improving the flexibility and reducing the adhesions in the shoulder joint, thus alleviating the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Equisetum arvense are used because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote joint mobility, which helps in alleviating symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of equisetum arvense rhizomes and roots from a clean location on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 40c for 2 hours in a food dehydrator.
Grind 10g of dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2g of the powder with 2g of gelatin in a small bowl to create a gelatin mixture.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately for storage.
